note.en: Constantine the Great dedicates the New Rome on the Bosporus. The empire is one, from Hispania to the Euphrates.
note.zh: 君士坦丁大帝在博斯普鲁斯海峡之滨为新罗马揭幕。此时帝国仍为一体，疆域西起伊比利亚，东抵幼发拉底河。

note.en: On the death of Theodosius I the empire is divided between his sons. The East, ruled from Constantinople, begins its own eleven-century story.
note.zh: 狄奥多西一世驾崩后，帝国由两子分治。以君士坦丁堡为都的东部，自此开启延续十一个世纪的历史。

note.en: Behind the new Theodosian Walls, the East weathers the storm of the Huns that breaks the West.
note.zh: 凭借新筑的狄奥多西城墙，东部帝国安然渡过了令西部倾覆的匈人风暴。

note.en: The Western throne is gone; the East endures. Anastasius fills the treasury and reforms the coinage.
note.zh: 西部帝位已成往事，东部依然屹立。阿纳斯塔修斯整顿币制，国库充盈。

note.en: A Latin-speaking soldier's nephew becomes emperor, dreaming of restoring the whole Roman world.
note.zh: 行伍出身的家族之侄登上帝位，胸怀恢复整个罗马世界的宏图。

note.en: Africa, Italy, and southern Spain are Roman again. The Mediterranean is once more, almost, a Roman lake.
note.zh: 阿非利加、意大利与西班牙南部重归罗马。地中海几乎再度成为罗马的内湖。

note.en: Lombards gnaw at Italy, Avars and Slavs press the Danube, yet the empire still spans three continents.
note.zh: 伦巴第人蚕食意大利，阿瓦尔人与斯拉夫人威逼多瑙河，但帝国仍横跨三洲。

note.en: Persia holds Egypt, Syria and Anatolia's heart; Avars besiege the capital. The empire survives by its walls, its fleet, and its faith.
note.zh: 波斯占据埃及、叙利亚与安纳托利亚腹地，阿瓦尔人兵临都城。帝国凭城墙、舰队与信念苦撑危局。

note.en: The True Cross returns to Jerusalem. Persia is broken — but both empires are exhausted, and a new power stirs in Arabia.
note.zh: 真十字架重归耶路撒冷，波斯一蹶不振——然而两大帝国俱已力竭，阿拉伯半岛上新的力量正在崛起。

note.en: In a single generation Syria, Palestine and Egypt are lost forever. The empire falls back on Anatolia and the sea.
note.zh: 仅仅一代人之间，叙利亚、巴勒斯坦与埃及永远地失去了。帝国退守安纳托利亚与海洋。

note.en: Leo III and Greek fire shatter the Caliphate's supreme effort against Constantinople. The tide of conquest breaks on the Theodosian Walls.
note.zh: 利奥三世与希腊火粉碎了哈里发国对君士坦丁堡的倾力一击，征服的浪潮在狄奥多西城墙下退去。

note.en: A leaner, militarized empire of themes holds Anatolia and the Aegean, torn within by the war over holy images.
note.zh: 军区制下更为精干的帝国固守安纳托利亚与爱琴海，内部却因圣像之争而撕裂。

note.en: Icons are restored for good. Crete and Sicily slip away to the Arabs, but learning and confidence revive in the capital.
note.zh: 圣像崇敬最终恢复。克里特与西西里渐落于阿拉伯人之手，但都城的学术与自信正在复苏。

note.en: Basil I seizes the throne, founding the dynasty under which the empire will climb back to greatness.
note.zh: 巴西尔一世夺取帝位，开创了引领帝国重返强盛的马其顿王朝。

note.en: Symeon of Bulgaria styles himself emperor and storms to the walls; the empire bends but does not break.
note.zh: 保加利亚的西美昂自称皇帝，兵锋直抵城下；帝国弯而不折。

note.en: Crete, Cyprus, Cilicia and Antioch are Roman again after three centuries. Young Basil II inherits a resurgent empire.
note.zh: 克里特、塞浦路斯、奇里乞亚与安条克在三个世纪后重归罗马。年轻的巴西尔二世继承了一个复兴中的帝国。

note.en: Basil II dies master of the Balkans to the Danube and Anatolia to Armenia — the strongest Roman state in four centuries.
note.zh: 巴西尔二世驾崩之时，帝国北抵多瑙河、东达亚美尼亚，是四百年来最强大的罗马国家。

note.en: The emperor is captured by the Seljuks; Bari, the last Italian city, falls to the Normans. The long crisis begins.
note.zh: 皇帝被塞尔柱人俘虏；最后的意大利城市巴里落入诺曼人之手。漫长的危机由此开始。

note.en: Anatolia is all but lost to the Turks. Alexios I Komnenos begins rebuilding from the Balkans and the coasts.
note.zh: 安纳托利亚几乎尽失于突厥人。阿莱克修斯一世·科穆宁自巴尔干与沿海之地开始重建。

note.en: Alexios and John II have clawed back the coasts of Anatolia. The empire is again the great power of the eastern Mediterranean.
note.zh: 阿莱克修斯与约翰二世收复了安纳托利亚沿海。帝国再度成为东地中海的强权。

note.en: Manuel I's empire reaches from Dalmatia to Cilicia, courted and resented by Latin West and Turkish East alike.
note.zh: 曼努埃尔一世的帝国西起达尔马提亚、东至奇里乞亚，令拉丁西方与突厥东方既仰慕又忌惮。

note.en: The Fourth Crusade sacks Constantinople. At Nicaea, the empire-in-exile keeps the Roman name and the Roman idea alive.
note.zh: 第四次十字军劫掠君士坦丁堡。流亡政权于尼西亚存续，守护着罗马之名与罗马的理念。

note.en: Michael VIII Palaiologos rides through the Golden Gate. Constantinople is Roman once more, though the empire is a shadow.
note.zh: 米海尔八世·帕列奥列格穿过金门入城。君士坦丁堡重归罗马，纵然帝国已是旧日之影。

note.en: Anatolia is lost to the Ottomans and the beyliks. The empire lives on in Thrace, Macedonia and the Morea — and in its art and letters.
note.zh: 安纳托利亚失于奥斯曼与诸侯国。帝国偏安于色雷斯、马其顿与摩里亚——亦存续于其艺术与学术之中。

note.en: Little remains but Constantinople itself and the Morea. Manuel II travels the courts of the West, seeking help that never comes.
note.zh: 所余无几，唯都城与摩里亚而已。曼努埃尔二世遍访西方宫廷，求援而终无所获。

note.en: On 29 May 1453 the Theodosian Walls are breached at last. Constantine XI dies fighting, and eleven centuries of the Eastern Roman Empire end.
note.zh: 1453年5月29日，狄奥多西城墙终被攻破。君士坦丁十一世战死城头，东罗马帝国十一个世纪的历史就此落幕。
